title: Key Points MCQ – Chapters 1 to 17 (Ajay Yadav)
source: Short Textbook of Anesthesia – Ajay Yadav (6th Edition) – Key Points

questions.question: What is the narrowest part of the airway in children up to the age of 6 years?
questions.explanation: Subglottis is the narrowest part in children up to the age of 6 years, however this does not act as deterrent to use cuffed tubes in children.

questions.question: During general anesthesia with muscle relaxants, the cords are held in which position?
questions.explanation: During general anesthesia with muscle relaxants the cords are held in cadaveric position.

questions.question: Chances of endobronchial intubation are more on which side due to shorter, wider and less acute angle?
questions.explanation: Due to shorter, wider and less acute angle chances of endobronchial intubation are more on right side.

questions.question: Aspiration in supine position most commonly involves which segment?

options.B: Apical segment of right lower lobe
options.C: Basal segment of left lower lobe

questions.explanation: Aspiration in supine position most commonly involves apical segment of right lower lobe.

questions.question: Where does maximum airway resistance to airflow occur?
questions.explanation: Maximum airway resistance to airflow occurs in trachea and then main bronchus and minimum in terminal bronchi.

questions.question: Endotracheal tubes and tracheostomy decrease anatomical dead space by:
questions.explanation: Endotracheal tubes and tracheostomy decrease the anatomical dead space by bypassing the upper airways.

questions.question: During general anesthesia, what happens to alveolar dead space?
questions.explanation: During general anesthesia alveolar dead space is increased.

questions.question: All inhalational agents blunt which response, thereby increasing shunt fraction?
questions.explanation: All inhalational agents blunt hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction (HPV) response thereby increasing the shunt fraction.

questions.question: The color of oxygen cylinder is:
questions.explanation: The color of oxygen cylinder is black body with white shoulders (top) and pressure is 2,000 pounds per square inch (psi).

questions.question: Nitrous oxide is stored in which color cylinders?
questions.explanation: Nitrous oxide is stored in blue color cylinders at a pressure of 760 psi.

questions.question: Entonox is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Entonox is the mixture of 50% oxygen and 50% nitrous oxide.

questions.question: Which end of the bobbin determines the flow rate in rotameter?
questions.explanation: Upper end of bobbin determines the flow rate in rotameter.

questions.question: Magill circuit is the circuit of choice for:
questions.explanation: Magill circuit is the circuit of choice for spontaneous ventilation. Fresh gas flow should be kept equal to minute volume to prevent rebreathing.

questions.question: Bain circuit is the circuit of choice for controlled ventilation. The fresh gas flow for controlled ventilation is:
questions.explanation: Bain circuit is the circuit of choice for controlled ventilation. Fresh gas flow for controlled ventilation is 1.6 times of minute ventilation.

questions.question: Jackson Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used semiclosed circuit for:
questions.explanation: Jackson Rees (Mapleson F) is the most commonly used semiclosed circuit for children.

questions.question: Sevoflurane by reacting with sodalime and barylime can produce:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane by reacting with sodalime and barylime can produce compound A.

questions.question: Which CO2 absorbent neither produces compound A nor carbon monoxide?
questions.explanation: Amsorb neither produces compound A nor carbon monoxide with desiccated sodalime and Barylime.

questions.question: The most common airway used to prevent tongue fall is:
questions.explanation: The most common airway used to prevent the tongue fall is Guedel's airway.

questions.question: Macintosh is the most commonly used:
questions.explanation: Macintosh is the most commonly used laryngoscope.

questions.question: Fiberoptic laryngoscope/bronchoscope-guided intubation is considered as:

options.C: Gold standard technique for difficult/failed intubation

questions.explanation: Fiberoptic laryngoscope/bronchoscope-guided intubation is considered as gold standard technique for the management of difficult/failed intubation.

questions.question: Among second generation LMA, which is most frequently used in India?
questions.explanation: Among the second generation LMA, I-Gel is most frequently used in India.

questions.question: The chief advantage of PVC endotracheal tube is:

options.C: Low pressure and high volume cuff

questions.explanation: The chief advantage of PVC tube is that their cuff is low pressure and high volume.

questions.question: Capnography is the surest sign to confirm:
questions.explanation: Capnography is the surest sign to confirm the correct position of endotracheal tube.

questions.question: During laryngoscopy, the correct head position involves:

options.A: Flexion at atlanto-occipital joint and extension at cervical spine
options.C: Extension at atlanto-occipital joint and flexion at cervical spine

questions.explanation: During laryngoscopy, there should be extension at atlanto-occipital joint and flexion at cervical spine.

questions.question: For solid food, how many hours of fasting is required preoperatively?
questions.explanation: For solid food 8 hours of fasting is must while clear fluids can be given up to 2 hours.

questions.question: MAO-A inhibitors should be stopped how long before surgery?
questions.explanation: MAO-A inhibitors should be stopped 3 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: Oral anticoagulants (Warfarin) should be stopped how many days before surgery?
questions.explanation: Oral anticoagulants (Warfarin) should be stopped 4 days prior to surgery.

questions.question: Clopidogrel should be stopped how many days prior to surgery?
questions.explanation: Antiplatelets except aspirin (Clopidogrel) should be stopped 5 days prior to surgery.

questions.question: Smoking should ideally be stopped before surgery for:
questions.explanation: Smoking should be stopped 8 weeks before surgery.

questions.question: Which medications require only the morning dose to be omitted on the day of surgery?

options.B: ACE inhibitors and angiotensin II antagonists

questions.explanation: ACE inhibitors and angiotensin II antagonist require only the morning dose to be omitted on the day of surgery.

questions.question: The three most important parameters for airway assessment are:

options.B: Mallampati grading, thyromental distance and neck movements
options.C: Mouth opening, teeth condition, and jaw size

questions.explanation: Mallampati grading, thyromental distance and neck movements are the three most important parameters to be assessed in every case.

questions.question: Every head injury should be considered to have which associated injury until proved otherwise?
questions.explanation: Every head injury should be considered to be having cervical injury until proved otherwise.

questions.question: The most preferred leads for ECG monitoring are:
questions.explanation: The most preferred leads for ECG monitoring are lead II and V5. Arrhythmia are best detected in lead II and ischemia's in lead V5.

questions.question: Maximum interval between two blood pressure recordings should not exceed:
questions.explanation: The maximum interval between two blood pressure recordings should not exceed more than 5 minutes.

questions.question: Gold standard monitoring of blood pressure is:
questions.explanation: Invasive blood pressure monitoring is considered as gold standard monitoring of blood pressure.

questions.question: The most commonly chosen artery for cannulation is:
questions.explanation: As radial artery has collaterals (transpalmar arch) for the blood supply of hand, it is most commonly chosen artery for cannulation.

questions.question: PAOP is best utilized to differentiate between:
questions.explanation: PAOP is best utilized to differentiate between cardiogenic and noncardiogenic pulmonary edema (ARDS).

questions.question: The best guide to assess tissue perfusion (cardiac output) is:

options.C: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood

questions.explanation: Oxygen saturation of mixed venous blood is best guide to assess tissue perfusion (cardiac output).

questions.question: Which monitor provides the best window for measuring cardiac functions in the intraoperative period?
questions.explanation: Transesophageal echocardiography provides the best window for measuring and assessing cardiac functions in intraoperative period.

questions.question: Each degree (°C) fall in temperature reduces the metabolic rate by:
questions.explanation: Each degree (°C) fall in temperature reduces the metabolic rate by 6 to 7%.

questions.question: The most commonly used muscle for neuromuscular monitoring is:

options.B: Adductor pollicis supplied by ulnar nerve

questions.explanation: Most commonly used muscle for neuromuscular monitoring is adductor pollicis supplied by ulnar nerve.

questions.question: Train of four is the most commonly used modality for:
questions.explanation: Train of four is the most commonly used modality for neuromuscular monitoring in clinical practice.

questions.question: BIS (Bispectral Index) monitor is the first scientifically validated monitor to assess:
questions.explanation: Bispectral index (BIS) monitor is the first scientifically validated and commercially available monitor to assess the depth of anesthesia.

questions.question: The most accurate measurement of core body temperature is provided by:
questions.explanation: Lower end of esophagus is considered as best site for core body temperature measurement. However, the most accurate measurement of core body temperature is provided by pulmonary artery.

questions.question: Crystalloids should be replaced in what ratio to intravascular volume loss?
questions.explanation: Crystalloids should be replaced in a ratio of 1.5:1 to intravascular volume loss while colloids in a ratio of 1:1.

questions.question: The most preferred fluid for maintenance as well as replacement is:
questions.explanation: Ringer lactate is the most preferred fluid for maintenance as well as replacement.

questions.question: The most acceptable trigger point for blood transfusion includes blood volume loss >20% and hemoglobin below:
questions.explanation: The most acceptable trigger points for blood transfusion are blood volume loss >20% and hemoglobin < 8 gm%.

questions.question: One unit of blood/packed cells increases hemoglobin by:
questions.explanation: One unit of blood/packed cells increases hemoglobin by 0.8%.

questions.question: Most common cause of mismatch transfusion is:
questions.explanation: Most common cause of mismatch transfusion is ABO incompatibility.

questions.question: Platelets are the only blood products stored at:
questions.explanation: Platelets are the only blood products which are stored at room temperature.

questions.question: The most common cause of death after massive transfusion is:
questions.explanation: DIC is the most common cause of death after massive transfusion while acute lung injury remains the leading cause of mortality after transfusion.

questions.question: Inhalational agents are mainly used in anesthesia for:
questions.explanation: Inhalational agents are mainly used in anesthesia for maintenance.

questions.question: The best estimate for determining the potency of inhalational agent is:
questions.explanation: Best estimate for determining the potency of inhalational agent is minimum alveolar concentration (MAC).

questions.question: Among agents used nowadays, induction is fastest with:
questions.explanation: Blood gas partition coefficient indicates induction and recovery. Among the agents used nowadays induction is fastest with desflurane and slowest with Halothane.

questions.question: Which inhalational agent can sensitize myocardium to adrenaline?
questions.explanation: Halothane (and theoretically other agents too except Isoflurane) can sensitize myocardium to adrenaline, exogenous to heart.

questions.question: Direct hepatocellular damage is seen with halothane but NOT with:
questions.explanation: Direct hepatocellular damage is seen with halothane (and theoretically with Isoflurane and Desflurane) but not with sevoflurane.

questions.question: Inflammable agents used in the past were:
questions.explanation: Inflammable agents used in past were Ether and Cyclopropane.

questions.question: Isoflurane is the agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Isoflurane is the agent of choice for cardiac patients.

questions.question: Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for induction in:
questions.explanation: Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for induction in children.

questions.question: Desflurane is agent of choice for all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Desflurane is agent of choice for prolonged surgeries, old age, renal diseases, obesity, day care surgery and shock. Sevoflurane is the agent of choice for hepatic patients.

questions.question: Nitrous oxide is absolutely contraindicated in all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Higher solubility of nitrous oxide than nitrogen makes it absolutely contraindicated in pneumothorax, pneumopericardium, pneumoperitoneum and pneumoencephalus.

questions.question: Minimum interval between two halothane exposures should be:
questions.explanation: Immunologic mechanism of halothane hepatitis warrants to avoid frequent use of halothane; at least an interval of 3 months is mandatory between two exposures.

questions.question: Most commonly used IV anesthetic for induction in current practice is:
questions.explanation: Most commonly used IV anesthetic for induction in current day practice is propofol.

questions.question: Consciousness after thiopentone is regained after 15-20 minutes due to:
questions.explanation: Although elimination half-life of Thiopentone is 10.4 hours but consciousness is regained after 15–20 minutes due to redistribution.

questions.question: Thiopentone is absolutely contraindicated in:
questions.explanation: Thiopentone is absolutely contraindicated in acute intermittent and variegate porphyria but can be used safely in porphyria cutanea tarda.

questions.question: Propofol is the IV agent of choice for:
questions.explanation: Propofol is the IV agent of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: The most cardiovascular stable IV agent among all is:
questions.explanation: Etomidate is the most cardiovascular stable among all IV agents.

questions.question: The major side effect of etomidate is:
questions.explanation: The major side effect of etomidate is adrenocortical suppression.

questions.question: Ketamine is the most potent analgesic among IV agents and the induction agent of choice for:

options.B: Cardiac patients with good LV function
options.C: Shock, full stomach, low cardiac output states

questions.explanation: Ketamine is the induction agent of choice for shock, full stomach, low cardiac output states and right to left shunts.

questions.question: Emergence reactions (hallucinations) are the major limiting factor for:
questions.explanation: Emergence reactions, mainly hallucinations, are the major limiting factor for restricting the use of ketamine.

questions.question: All pressures (intraocular, intragastric, intracranial) are raised by:
questions.explanation: All pressures like intraocular, intragastric, intracranial are raised by ketamine.

questions.question: The most important receptors for the action of opioids are:
questions.explanation: The most important receptors for the action of opioids are mu receptors.

questions.question: Muscle rigidity (Wooden chest syndrome) is most commonly seen with:
questions.explanation: Muscle rigidity (Wooden chest syndrome) is most commonly seen with alfentanil.

questions.question: Tolerance is seen to all actions of opioids EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Tolerance is seen to all actions of opioids except constipation and miosis.

questions.question: Sufentanil is the agent of choice for inhibiting stress response to:
questions.explanation: Sufentanil is the agent of choice for inhibiting stress response to laryngoscopy and intubation.

questions.question: Remifentanil is the opioid of choice for:
questions.explanation: Remifentanil is the opioid of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Dexmedetomidine is a new:
questions.explanation: Dexmedetomidine is a new α2 agonist which is frequently used in anesthetic practice.

questions.question: The ideal muscle relaxant for intubation due to early onset and short duration is:
questions.explanation: Because of early onset and short duration Succinylcholine is the ideal muscle relaxant for intubation.

questions.question: The most prominent effect of suxamethonium is:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia is the most prominent effect of suxamethonium.

questions.question: Succinylcholine is the most commonly implicated drug for:
questions.explanation: Succinylcholine is the most commonly implicated drug for malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Fading after suxamethonium is pathognomonic of:
questions.explanation: Fading after suxamethonium is pathognomonic of phase II block.

questions.question: The most cardiovascular stable muscle relaxant is:
questions.explanation: Vecuronium is most cardiovascular stable muscle relaxant.

questions.question: Rocuronium is the nondepolarizer of choice for:
questions.explanation: Rocuronium is nondepolarizer of choice for intubation.

questions.question: Atracurium and cisatracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation making them the relaxant of choice for:
questions.explanation: Atracurium and cisatracurium are metabolized by Hoffman degradation making them relaxant of choice for hepatic and renal failure.

questions.question: Mivacurium is the muscle relaxant of choice for:
questions.explanation: Mivacurium is the muscle relaxant of choice for day care surgery.

questions.question: Sugammadex can rapidly reverse the block by steroidal nondepolarizers but CANNOT reverse:
questions.explanation: Sugammadex rapidly and completely reverses the block by steroidal type of nondepolarizers but cannot reverse benzylisoquinoline compounds.

questions.question: Train of four ratio that guarantees recovery from neuromuscular block is:
questions.explanation: Train of four ratio > 0.9 guarantees recovery.

questions.question: More than 80% of cardiac arrest occurs at the time of:
questions.explanation: Maximum complications are reported in maintenance period however more than 80% of cardiac arrest occurs at the time of induction.

questions.question: The most common cause of anesthesia related death in perioperative period is:
questions.explanation: Postoperative respiratory depression has been cited as the most common cause of anesthesia related death in perioperative period.

questions.question: The single most important predisposing factor for aspiration is:
questions.explanation: Full stomach is the single most important predisposing factor for aspiration.

questions.question: The most common postoperative pulmonary complication is:
questions.explanation: Atelectasis is the most common postoperative pulmonary complication and the most common cause of atelectasis is secretions.

questions.question: The most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Secretions is the most common cause of laryngospasm in anesthesia.

questions.question: The most common arrhythmia seen in the perioperative period is:
questions.explanation: Tachycardia is the most common arrhythmia seen in perioperative period.

questions.question: The most common cause of hypotension in the perioperative period is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of hypotension in perioperative period is intravascular volume depletion.

questions.question: The most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Brachial plexus injuries are the most common postoperative nerve injury associated with general anesthesia followed by ulnar nerve.

questions.question: Pain is the most common complication in the:
questions.explanation: Pain is the most common complication is postoperative period.

questions.question: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis in anesthesia, followed by:
questions.explanation: Muscle relaxants are the most common cause of anaphylaxis followed by latex allergy.

questions.question: Cautery is responsible for what percentage of fires seen in OT?
questions.explanation: Cautery is responsible for 68% of fires seen in OT.

questions.question: Hyperkalemia induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in:
questions.explanation: Hyperkalemia induced ventricular fibrillation is the most common cause of death in malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting local anesthetic, and the longest acting is:
questions.explanation: Chloroprocaine is the shortest acting and dibucaine is the longest acting local anesthetic.

questions.question: Maximum systemic absorption of local anesthetic has been seen after:
questions.explanation: Maximum systemic absorption has been seen after intercostal nerve block.

questions.question: The first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity is:
questions.explanation: Central nervous system is the first system involved in local anesthetic toxicity.

questions.question: All local anesthetics at clinically used doses are vasodilators EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: At clinically used doses all local anesthetics are vasodilators except cocaine, levobupivacaine and ropivacaine.

questions.question: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with:
questions.explanation: Methemoglobinemia is usually seen with prilocaine however benzocaine can also cause methemoglobinemia.

questions.question: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline is:
questions.explanation: Maximum safe dose of lignocaine without adrenaline is 4.5 mg/kg (maximum 300 mg) while with adrenaline it is 7 mg/kg (maximum 500 mg).

questions.question: Because of high cardiotoxicity, which drug is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block?
questions.explanation: Because of high cardiotoxicity bupivacaine is absolutely contraindicated for Bier's block.

questions.question: The drug of choice for treatment of bupivacaine-induced ventricular tachycardia is:
questions.explanation: Amiodarone now a days is considered as drug of choice for the treatment of bupivacaine (and other local anesthetic) induced ventricular tachycardia.

questions.question: For spinal and epidural anesthesia, which preparations should be used?
questions.explanation: For spinal and epidural anesthesia preservative free preparations should be used.

questions.question: The use of ultrasound has revolutionized the practice of:
questions.explanation: The use of ultrasound has not only made the nerve blocks technically easy but also has improved the precision.

questions.question: Ulnar nerve is usually spared with which approach of brachial plexus block?
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve is usually spared with interscalene approach while musculocutaneous is usually spared with axillary approach.

questions.question: The most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block is:
questions.explanation: The most bothersome complication of supraclavicular block is pneumothorax.

questions.question: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for:
questions.explanation: Cervical plexus block is best utilized for carotid endarterectomy.

questions.question: The line joining the highest point on iliac crest corresponds to:

options.C: L4 and L5 interspace or L4 spine

questions.explanation: The line joining the highest point on iliac crest corresponds to L4 and L5 interspace or L4 spine.

questions.question: Spinal cord extends to lower border of which vertebra in adults?
questions.explanation: Spinal cord extends from medulla oblongata to lower border of L1 in adults and L3 in infants.

questions.question: The sequence of blockade after central neuraxial block is:
questions.explanation: The sequence of blockade after CNB is autonomic > sensory > motor.

questions.question: The most common complication of spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Hypotension is the most common complication of spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: The most common cause of nausea and vomiting during spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: The most common cause of nausea and vomiting is hypotension.

questions.question: The most commonly used drug for spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Hyperbaric bupivacaine is the most commonly used drug for spinal.

questions.question: The most preferred vasopressor for treatment of spinal hypotension is:
questions.explanation: Ephedrine is the most preferred vasopressor for the treatment of spinal hypotension.

questions.question: The drug of choice for treatment of shivering is:
questions.explanation: The drug of choice for treatment of shivering is pethidine.

questions.question: Postural relation (pain in sitting/standing, relieved by lying down) is diagnostic of:
questions.explanation: Postural relation, i.e. pain in sitting and standing position and getting relieved in lying down is diagnostic of postspinal headache.

questions.question: The most commonly involved cranial nerve after spinal anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: 6th is most commonly involved cranial nerve after spinal anesthesia.

questions.question: The most common organism responsible for meningitis after spinal is:
questions.explanation: Streptococcus viridans has been cited as the most common organism responsible for meningitis after spinal while Staphylococcus epidermidis is the most common organism responsible for meningitis after epidural.

questions.question: Transient neurological symptoms and cauda equina syndrome led to almost elimination of which drug for spinal?
questions.explanation: Transient neurological symptoms and cauda equina syndrome lead to almost elimination of lignocaine for spinal.

questions.question: The most commonly used needle for epidural is:
questions.explanation: The most commonly used needle for epidural is Tuohy needle and most commonly used method to locate epidural space is by loss of resistance technique.

questions.question: The most commonly used combination for postoperative analgesia and painless labor via epidural is:
questions.explanation: Fentanyl + bupivacaine or ropivacaine as continuous infusion is the most commonly used combination for postoperative analgesia and painless labor.

questions.question: It is considered safe to give CNB how many hours after last dose of LMWH?
questions.explanation: It is considered safe to give central neuraxial block 12 hours after last dose of LMWH and LMWH can be given safely 6 hours after central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: Patients with platelet count more than which value can safely receive CNB?
questions.explanation: Patients with platelet count more than 80,000/cubic mm can be safely given central neuraxial blocks.

questions.question: CNB should be delayed for how many days after clopidogrel?
questions.explanation: CNB should be delayed for 7 days after clopidogrel.

questions.question: Pin index system prevents wrong fitting of:
questions.explanation: Pin index system is to prevent the wrong fitting of cylinders while diameter index safety system is to prevent the wrong fitting of central supply pipe lines.

questions.question: Aladin cassette vaporizer can be used to deliver:
questions.explanation: Aladin cassette vaporizer is the latest vaporizer, which can be used to deliver all inhalational agents.

questions.question: The most commonly used video laryngoscope is:
questions.explanation: Most commonly used video laryngoscopes is C-MAC.

questions.question: Definitive airway management devices include:
questions.explanation: Definitive airway management devices includes endotracheal tube and tracheostomy.

questions.question: Clear fluids can be given up to how many hours before surgery?
questions.explanation: For solid food 8 hours of fasting is must while clear fluids can be given up to 2 hours.

questions.question: Herbal medications should be stopped how long before surgery?
questions.explanation: All herbal medications should be stopped 7 days before surgery.

questions.question: Co-oximeters can differentiate between:
questions.explanation: Co-oximeters are the special type of oximeters which can differentiate between normal and abnormal hemoglobin.

questions.question: Temperature should be monitored in patients receiving GA lasting more than:
questions.explanation: Temperature should be monitored in patients receiving general anesthesia >30 minutes and in all patients whose surgery lasts > 1 hour.

questions.question: Fading, post-tetanic facilitation and post-tetanic count are only exhibited by:
questions.explanation: Fading, post-tetanic facilitation and post-tetanic count are only exhibited by non-depolarizing muscle relaxants.

questions.question: Children are prone for fluid overload and should receive what fraction of calculated dose?
questions.explanation: Children are prone for fluid overload therefore should receive 2/3rd of the calculated dose.

questions.question: The most widely used technique for autologous blood donation is:
questions.explanation: Normovolemic/isovolemic hemodilution is the most widely used technique for autologous blood donation.

questions.question: Ondansetron/Granisetron remains the drugs of choice for:
questions.explanation: Ondansetron/Granisetron remains the drugs of choice for treatment as well as prophylaxis for postoperative nausea and vomiting.

questions.question: Propofol and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants can delay or prevent:
questions.explanation: Propofol and non-depolarizing muscle relaxants can delay or prevent malignant hyperthermia.

questions.question: Allergic reactions are common with ester local anesthetics but rare with:
questions.explanation: Allergic reactions are common with esters but rare with amides.

questions.question: Local anesthetics with adrenaline should NOT be used for ring block of fingers, toes, penis or pinna because:

options.B: They are ineffective at these sites
options.C: These structures have end arteries and necrosis can occur

questions.explanation: Local anesthetics with adrenaline can cause necrosis and gangrene if used for ring block of fingers, toes, penis or pinna because these structures have end arteries.

questions.question: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer which is absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy because:
questions.explanation: Gallamine is the only nondepolarizer which can cross the placenta therefore absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy.

questions.question: Onset of a nondepolarizer is inversely proportional to:
questions.explanation: Onset of a nondepolarizer is inversely proportional to potency.

questions.question: Rapid sequence induction technique must be employed for patients at high risk of:
questions.explanation: Rapid sequence induction technique must be employed for giving GA to the patients who are at high risk of aspiration.

questions.question: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce:
questions.explanation: Nitroglycerin as continuous infusion is the most commonly used agent to produce controlled hypotension.

questions.question: Apnea after spinal anesthesia is usually due to:
questions.explanation: Apnea after spinal anesthesia is usually due to severe hypotension.

questions.question: The most common thermal perturbation seen during anesthesia is:
questions.explanation: Hypothermia is the most common thermal perturbation seen during anesthesia.
